act.long_title: An Act to define and amend the law relating to certain kinds of specific relief

nodes.title: Short title, extent and commencement
nodes.summary: Act extends to whole of India except Jammu & Kashmir (pre-370 abrogation). Commenced on 1 March 1964.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: Defines 'obligation', 'settlement', 'trust', 'trustee' for purposes of this Act.

cross_links.reason: Obligation under Contract Act underpins the defined term 'obligation' here

nodes.title: Recovery of specific immovable property
nodes.summary: A person entitled to specific immovable property may recover it in the manner provided by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

nodes.title: Suit by person dispossessed of immovable property
nodes.summary: Person dispossessed of immovable property without consent may sue to recover possession within 6 months, regardless of title.

nodes.title: Recovery of specific movable property
nodes.summary: Trustee or other person holding movable property in a special capacity may be compelled to deliver it.

nodes.title: Liability of person holding property as agent or trustee
nodes.summary: When person holds property as agent, trustee or in any other fiduciary capacity and wrongfully transfers it, buyer/transferee with notice is compelled to deliver it.

cross_links.reason: Agent's authority and fiduciary duty defined in Contract Act

nodes.title: Specific performance of contracts enforceable
nodes.summary: Specific performance of a contract shall be enforced by the court subject to provisions of the Act. Post-2018 amendment: specific performance is now a general rule, not discretionary.
nodes.amendment_note: Amended by Amendment Act 2018 — changed from discretionary to rule-based enforcement

cross_links.reason: Contract must be valid under Contract Act to be specifically enforced here
Alternative remedy of damages under Contract Act; courts weigh before granting specific performance

nodes.title: Cases in which specific performance of contracts connected with trusts enforceable
nodes.summary: Specific performance of an act relating to a trust or a contract to create a trust is enforceable.

nodes.title: Specific performance of part of contract
nodes.summary: Court may decree specific performance of part of a contract where the part not performed is small and can be compensated in money, or where performance of rest is not possible.

cross_links.reason: Monetary compensation for the unperformed part governed by Contract Act §73

nodes.title: Rights of purchaser or lessee against persons with no title or imperfect title
nodes.summary: Where vendor/lessor contracts for property they do not own or have imperfect title, purchaser may compel vendor to convey what they can, with compensation.

nodes.title: Contracts not specifically enforceable
nodes.summary: Lists contracts not specifically enforceable: (a) compensation in money adequate substitute; (b) contracts running into minute detail; (c) contracts personal in nature; (d) contracts dependent on personal qualifications; (e) determinable contracts.
nodes.amendment_note: Substantially amended in 2018 — removed judicial discretion category

cross_links.reason: When specific performance denied, damages under §73 Contract Act become the remedy
Determinable contracts also overlap with frustrated contracts under §56

nodes.title: Who may obtain specific performance
nodes.summary: Party to contract, their representative in interest, or where contract is made by a company in its pre-incorporation stage — the company after incorporation.

cross_links.reason: Assignability of contractual rights — who steps into shoes of original party

nodes.title: Personal bars to relief
nodes.summary: Specific performance cannot be granted to plaintiff who: (a) has become incapable of performing the contract, (b) has violated any essential term, (c) acts inequitably, or (d) fails to aver readiness and willingness to perform.

cross_links.reason: Refusal of party to perform = repudiation under Contract Act §39; bars specific performance here
Performance at specified time — delay by plaintiff bars relief under this section

nodes.title: Contract to sell or let property by one who has no title
nodes.summary: Specific performance not granted to vendor with no title at time of suit unless vendor undertakes to procure title before performance.

nodes.title: Non-enforcement except with variation
nodes.summary: Where parties to a contract have erred regarding existence of a fact, court may enforce contract with variation to correct the error.

cross_links.reason: Mistake of fact under Contract Act — bilateral mistake may affect validity; §18 SRA provides variation remedy

nodes.title: Relief against parties and persons claiming under them by subsequent title
nodes.summary: Specific performance may be enforced against party to contract, any other party claiming through them, unless they are bona fide purchasers for value without notice.

nodes.title: Substituted performance of contract
nodes.summary: Post-2018: Aggrieved party may get contract performed by third party or agency at cost of defaulting party. Must give prior notice of 30 days.
nodes.amendment_note: New section introduced by 2018 Amendment Act — a significant practical remedy

cross_links.reason: Cost of substituted performance recoverable as damages under §73 Contract Act

nodes.title: Power to award compensation in certain cases
nodes.summary: Court granting or refusing specific performance may award compensation to plaintiff in addition to or instead of specific performance.

cross_links.reason: Compensation principles mirror §73 Contract Act — party placed in position as if contract performed
Liquidated damages under §74 may also be awarded in lieu of specific performance

nodes.title: Power to grant relief for possession, partition, refund of earnest money, etc.
nodes.summary: Court may, in addition to specific performance decree, grant possession, partition, or refund of earnest money where contract fails.

nodes.title: Liquidation of damages not a bar to specific performance
nodes.summary: Sum named in contract as amount to be paid in case of breach does not per se bar specific performance — merely creates a collateral obligation.

cross_links.reason: Penalty/liquidated damages clause in §74 Contract Act — SRA §23 clarifies it doesn't bar specific relief

nodes.title: Bar of suit for compensation for breach after dismissal of suit for specific performance
nodes.summary: If a suit for specific performance is dismissed, a subsequent suit for compensation for the same breach is barred (res judicata principle).

cross_links.reason: Damages under §73 Contract Act — cannot sue for these after failed specific performance suit on same breach

nodes.title: Application of preceding sections to instruments and awards
nodes.summary: Sections 10–24 apply to instruments and awards as they apply to contracts.

nodes.title: When instrument may be rectified
nodes.summary: Instrument may be rectified where, through fraud or mutual mistake, it does not express real intention of the parties.

cross_links.reason: Fraud as defined in Contract Act §17 is one of the grounds for rectification under SRA §26
Bilateral mistake under §20 Contract Act is ground for rectification here

nodes.title: When cancellation may be ordered
nodes.summary: Any person who apprehends that a written instrument may cause injury if left outstanding may sue for cancellation. Court has discretion.

cross_links.reason: Void and voidable agreements under Contract Act §2 — voidable contracts eligible for cancellation here
Voidability on grounds of coercion/fraud/misrepresentation under §19 Contract Act

nodes.title: Rescission in certain circumstances of contracts for sale or lease of immovable property
nodes.summary: Court may rescind contracts for sale/lease of immovable property on failure of consideration or mutual mistake.

cross_links.reason: Obligation to restore benefit on rescission — governed by §65 Contract Act

nodes.title: Cancellation of instrument after specific performance decreed
nodes.summary: After decree of specific performance, court may cancel any document which has become void or voidable.

nodes.title: Court may require party rescinding to do equity
nodes.summary: On adjudging cancellation court may require party claiming cancellation to restore any benefit received and do equity.

cross_links.reason: §65 Contract Act — restoration of benefit received under void/voidable contract

nodes.title: Discretion of court as to declaration of status or right
nodes.summary: Court may make binding declaration of right where plaintiff is entitled to such declaration — but the declaration alone without consequential relief must be sufficient.

nodes.title: Effect of declaration
nodes.summary: Declaration made under §34 is binding only on parties to the suit and persons claiming through them.

nodes.title: Preventive relief how granted
nodes.summary: Preventive relief (injunction) granted at discretion of court — either temporary or perpetual.

nodes.title: Temporary and perpetual injunctions
nodes.summary: Temporary injunction continues until specified time or further court order; perpetual injunction restrains party forever from asserting a right or doing an act.

nodes.title: Perpetual injunction when granted
nodes.summary: Perpetual injunction granted to prevent breach of obligation — where obligation arises from contract. Damages must be inadequate.

cross_links.reason: Inadequacy of damages under Contract Act §73 is the test for granting perpetual injunction

nodes.title: Mandatory injunctions
nodes.summary: Court may compel performance of an act by mandatory injunction to prevent breach of obligation or undo breach already committed.

nodes.title: Damages in lieu of or in addition to injunction
nodes.summary: Court may award damages in lieu of or in addition to injunction. Claim for damages does not disentitle plaintiff from injunction.

cross_links.reason: Damages quantified per Contract Act §73 principles even when injunction is the primary relief

nodes.title: Injunction when refused
nodes.summary: Injunction cannot be granted: (a) to stay judicial proceedings already started; (b) to prevent legislative acts; (c) for breach of contract not specifically enforceable; (d) to prevent nuisance not clearly established; (e) where plaintiff delayed (laches).

cross_links.reason: Contracts in restraint of trade under §27 Contract Act — injunction to enforce such contracts refused under §41(b) SRA

nodes.title: Injunction to perform negative agreement
nodes.summary: Where contract contains positive and negative stipulations, court may grant injunction to enforce the negative covenant even where it cannot decree specific performance of the positive covenant.
nodes.amendment_note: Key provision used in employment and NDA contexts for enforcement of non-compete/non-disclosure clauses

cross_links.reason: Restraint of trade §27 — §42 SRA allows injunction on negative covenants even where §27 makes the positive part unenforceable as restraint of trade
